entry.title: Auth0
entry.description: Auth0 provides authentication and authorization as a service for web, mobile, and legacy applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 7,000 active users per month with core authentication features.
caveats: No SLA or enterprise support on free tier
Advanced features require paid plans

useCases: Add secure login to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Auth0 account.
Create a new application in the Auth0 dashboard.
Configure allowed callback URLs for your app.
Install the Auth0 SDK for your platform.
Integrate Auth0 authentication into your app using provided guides.
entry.whenToUse: Use Auth0 when you need to quickly add secure, standards-compliant authentication to your applications without building your own auth system.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Auth0 if you require full on-premises control, need advanced customization, or expect to exceed free tier limits without budget.
entry.body: Auth0 offers a robust authentication platform with a generous free tier, making it easy to add secure login and user management to your apps.

entry.title: Authgear
entry.description: Authgear provides user authentication, social login, and identity management as a service for web and mobile apps.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 1,000 monthly active users with core authentication features.
caveats: No custom domain on free tier
Limited to basic email templates

useCases: Add authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Authgear account.
Create a new application in the Authgear dashboard.
Configure authentication methods (email/password, social logins).
Integrate Authgear SDK into your app using provided documentation.
Test user signup and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authgear when you need quick, secure authentication and social login for web or mobile apps without building your own auth system.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced customizations, on-premise deployment, or need support for more than 1,000 monthly active users on the free tier.
entry.body: Authgear offers a developer-friendly authentication platform with a generous free tier, making it easy to add secure login and identity management to your apps.

entry.title: Authress
entry.description: Authress provides authentication and authorization as a service for modern applications with fine-grained access control.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 100 monthly active users with core authentication and authorization features.
caveats: No SAML SSO in free tier
Support limited to community channels

useCases: Add secure authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Authress account at https://authress.io/.
Create a new application in the Authress dashboard.
Configure your application's callback URLs and authentication settings.
Integrate the Authress SDK or API with your app.
Test user login and access control flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authress when you need to quickly add secure authentication and fine-grained authorization to your application without managing your own auth infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require SAML SSO, advanced enterprise integrations, or need to support more than 100 monthly active users for free.
avoidIf: expect high user volumes on free tier
entry.body: Authress offers a developer-friendly authentication and authorization platform with a generous free tier for small projects, enabling secure user management and access control without complex setup.

entry.title: Authy
entry.description: Authy provides two-factor authentication (2FA) services for securing user logins and sensitive actions.

freeTierDetails.summary: Authy offers a free tier with limited authentications per month.
caveats: Requires Twilio account
SMS/voice may incur carrier fees
API usage beyond free tier is billed
billingRiskNotes: API calls above free tier are charged at standard Twilio rates

useCases: Add 2FA to web or mobile apps
Secure sensitive actions with step-up authentication
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Twilio account and enable Authy.
Create a new Authy application in the Twilio Console.
Integrate the Authy API into your app using official SDKs.
Register users and enable 2FA via SMS, voice, or app-based OTP.
Test authentication flows and monitor usage in the Twilio Console.
entry.whenToUse: Use Authy when you need to quickly add 2FA to your app with minimal setup and broad device support.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need unlimited free authentications, on-premise deployment, or want to avoid external dependencies.
entry.body: Authy by Twilio enables developers to add secure two-factor authentication to applications using SMS, voice, or app-based OTP, with a generous free tier for low-volume use.

entry.title: Azure Active Directory B2C
entry.description: Azure AD B2C provides cloud-based identity and access management for customer-facing applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for up to 50,000 monthly active users (MAU) with basic authentication features.
caveats: Premium features and custom policies incur extra charges
External identity providers may have additional costs
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if MAU exceeds 50,000 or premium features are enabled

useCases: Add user sign-up and sign-in to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal and create an Azure AD B2C tenant.
Link your Azure subscription to the B2C tenant.
Register your application in the B2C tenant.
Configure user flows (sign-up, sign-in, password reset).
Integrate the application using the provided endpoints and libraries.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, managed authentication for consumer-facing apps with social or enterprise identity providers.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premises-only identity, advanced custom policy support without extra cost, or need to support more than 50,000 free MAU.
entry.body: Azure Active Directory B2C enables secure, scalable authentication for customer-facing applications, supporting social and enterprise identity providers with a generous free tier.

entry.title: Firebase Auth
entry.description: Firebase Auth provides authentication services for web and mobile apps with support for multiple identity providers.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes generous monthly quota for authentication operations.
limits: 10k verifications/month for email/password, anonymous, and social providers
caveats: SMS verification quota is low and overages are billed.
Enterprise SSO and phone auth have stricter limits.
billingRiskNotes: SMS overages can incur charges if quota is exceeded.

useCases: Add user authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project in the Firebase Console.
Enable Authentication and select desired sign-in methods.
Add Firebase SDK to your app (web, iOS, or Android).
Configure authentication providers in your app code.
Test user sign-up and sign-in flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need quick, secure authentication for web or mobile apps with minimal backend setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced enterprise SSO, custom auth flows, or need to avoid vendor lock-in.
entry.body: Firebase Auth offers a simple way to add authentication to apps, supporting multiple providers and generous free tier limits for most use cases.

entry.title: LoginLlama
entry.description: LoginLlama provides suspicious login detection and fraud prevention via a simple API integration for your authentication flows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1,000 login checks per month with all core features.
caveats: No SLA on free tier
Branding may be visible

useCases: Detect suspicious logins in web or mobile apps
Add fraud prevention to authentication flows
Protect user accounts from credential stuffing
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free LoginLlama account.
Create a project and obtain your API key.
Integrate the LoginLlama API in your authentication backend.
Send login events to the API and review risk scores.
Act on suspicious logins based on API responses.
entry.whenToUse: Use LoginLlama when you need to quickly add suspicious login detection to your app without building your own risk engine.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require on-premise deployment, advanced customization, or need to process more than 1,000 logins monthly for free.
entry.body: LoginLlama offers a simple API to detect suspicious login attempts and prevent fraud, suitable for small projects and early-stage apps.

entry.title: PropelAuth
entry.description: PropelAuth provides authentication, user management, and multi-tenant support for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes authentication and user management for up to 1,000 monthly active users.
caveats: No custom domains on free tier
Limited to basic branding
Advanced SSO and audit logs require paid plans

useCases: Add authentication to a SaaS app
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a PropelAuth account.
Create a new project in the dashboard.
Configure authentication methods (email/password, social logins, SSO).
Integrate the SDK into your app using provided documentation.
Test user signup and login flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use PropelAuth when you need quick, secure authentication and multi-tenant support without building from scratch.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced customization, on-premise deployment, or need features only available on paid plans.
entry.body: PropelAuth offers a developer-friendly authentication service with built-in user management and multi-tenant support, ideal for SaaS and modern web apps.

entry.title: SimpleLogin
entry.description: SimpleLogin provides email aliasing and forwarding to protect your real email address from spam and phishing.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes 10 active aliases, unlimited bandwidth, and open-source self-hosting option.
caveats: No custom domain support on free plan
Limited to 1 mailbox

useCases: Protecting your real email from spam
Signing up for online services safely
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free SimpleLogin account at simplelogin.io.
Verify your email address.
Create your first email alias from the dashboard.
Forward emails from the alias to your real inbox.
Reply to emails using your alias if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use SimpleLogin when you want to protect your real email address from spam or manage multiple identities easily.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need more than 10 aliases, custom domain support, or advanced enterprise email features.
avoidIf: require custom domain on free plan
entry.body: SimpleLogin is an open-source email aliasing service that helps users protect their real email address and manage multiple identities with ease.

entry.title: Stack Auth
entry.description: Stack Auth provides passwordless authentication and user management APIs for web and mobile applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic passwordless authentication and user management for small projects.
caveats: No enterprise SSO on free tier
Limited support
Custom domains require paid plan

useCases: Add passwordless login to web apps
Enable social login for mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Stack Auth account.
Create a new application in the dashboard.
Configure allowed redirect URLs and authentication methods.
Integrate the SDK or API into your app.
Test authentication flow in your development environment.
entry.whenToUse: Use Stack Auth when you need quick, passwordless authentication and user management for small to medium web or mobile apps.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced enterprise features, custom domains on free tier, or need on-premises deployment.
entry.body: Stack Auth offers a simple way to add passwordless authentication and user management to your applications, with a generous free tier for small projects.

entry.title: Supabase Auth
entry.description: Supabase Auth provides user authentication and authorization for web and mobile apps with social logins and email/password support.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes up to 50,000 monthly active users with core authentication features.
caveats: Enterprise SSO and advanced MFA require paid plans
Email/SMS delivery subject to provider limits

useCases: Add authentication to web or mobile apps
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Supabase account.
Create a new Supabase project.
Navigate to the Auth section in the dashboard.
Configure authentication providers (e.g., email, Google).
Integrate Supabase Auth SDK in your app.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need fast, easy authentication for web or mobile apps with social login and email/password support.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require enterprise SSO, advanced MFA, or need to self-host authentication infrastructure.
entry.body: Supabase Auth is a managed authentication service supporting social logins, email/password, and passwordless flows, ideal for quickly securing apps without complex setup.
